title: SAP Finance Functional Architect (Remote, Full-Time) [HRPP]
description_text:
About Smart Working

At Smart Working, we believe your job should not only look right on paper but also feel right every day. This isn’t just another remote opportunity - it’s about finding where you truly belong, no matter where you are. From day one, you’re welcomed into a genuine community that values your growth and well-being.

Our mission is simple: to break down geographic barriers and connect skilled professionals with outstanding global teams and products for full-time, long-term roles. We help you discover meaningful work with teams that invest in your success, where you’re empowered to grow personally and professionally.
Join one of the highest-rated workplaces on Glassdoor and experience what it means to thrive in a truly remote-first world.

About the role

As an SAP Finance Functional Architect, you will be responsible for defining, governing, and optimising the end-to-end SAP Finance solution architecture across the enterprise. This is a senior, strategic role with design authority over core Finance processes, ensuring scalable, standardised, and compliant SAP S/4HANA Finance solutions that align with long-term transformation objectives.

You will act as the primary functional authority for Finance, working closely with SAP Product teams, Centres of Excellence, Business Process Owners, and transformation programmes to guide solution decisions across Order-to-Cash (OtC), Record-to-Report (RtR), and Procure-to-Pay (PtP).
\n

Responsibilities
Own the end-to-end SAP Finance functional architecture across OtC, RtR, and PtP.
Translate business strategy and process requirements into scalable SAP S/4HANA Finance solutions.
Maintain the Finance solution blueprint, global standards, and functional guardrails.
Ensure alignment with enterprise architecture principles and SAP best practices.
Establish and enforce global Finance design standards.
Govern changes to Finance processes through formal design authority forums.
Drive process harmonisation across operating entities to maximise reuse and minimise customisation.
Provide expert guidance on SAP Finance capabilities and best-practice process design.
Partner with SAP Product Managers and Centres of Excellence to ensure consistent solution design and backlog prioritisation.
Support product teams during refinement, solutioning, configuration, and functional testing.
Review and approve functional specifications, configuration decisions, and enhancement requests.
Act as functional lead across major initiatives including S/4HANA, Central Finance, Group Reporting, Tax, Treasury, and BTP extensions.
Support programme streams with solution design, data mapping, integrations, testing, and cutover activities.
Identify and resolve functional dependencies and design risks.
Ensure Finance master data, transactional structures, and reporting hierarchies align with global standards.
Oversee functional aspects of integrations across SAP and non-SAP systems (e.g. CPI, APIs, banking platforms, tax engines).
Support the design of analytics, KPIs, group reporting, and compliance-related reporting structures.
Act as a trusted functional advisor to Finance leadership, Global Process Owners, and business SMEs.
Facilitate solution design workshops and capability assessments.
Ensure functional designs meet quality, audit, control, and compliance requirements.
Support testing cycles (SIT, UAT, regression), training validation, and business readiness activities.

Requirements
Deep SAP Finance functional expertise across Order-to-Cash, Record-to-Report, and Procure-to-Pay.
Strong knowledge of SAP S/4HANA Finance, including: Universal Journal, Financial Accounting (FI), Management Accounting (CO), Asset Accounting, Group Reporting
Proven experience in functional architecture or senior functional design roles.
Strong understanding of SAP best-practice processes and end-to-end cross-functional integration.
Experience operating in complex, multi-entity, global transformation environments.
Excellent stakeholder management, communication, and leadership skills.
Ability to act as a design authority and make strategic, governance-driven decisions.

Nice to have
Experience with SAP RISE, Central Finance, BTP, BRF+, tax engines, or Treasury solutions.
Exposure to regulatory, audit, and internal control frameworks (e.g. SOX, IFRS).
SAP Finance certifications.
Experience working in regulated, multi-market, or highly governed organisations.

Benefits
Fixed Shifts: 12:00 PM - 9:30 PM IST (Summer) | 1:00 PM - 10:30 PM IST (Winter)
No Weekend Work: Real work-life balance, not just words
Day 1 Benefits: Laptop and full medical insurance provided
Support That Matters:Mentorship, community, and forums where ideas are shared
True Belonging: A long-term career where your contributions are valued

\n
At Smart Working, you’ll never be just another remote hire.

Be a Smart Worker - valued, empowered, and part of a culture that celebrates integrity, excellence, and ambition.

If that sounds like your kind of place, we’d love to hear your story.

title: Ultrasound Technologist
description_text:
Join the fastest growing outpatient radiology practice in the Nation- SimonMed Imaging! Our commitment to excellence and improving patient care paired with the best-in-class technology allows us to be an industry leader in the constantly evolving health care environment. Secure your spot now and take advantage of a unique career opportunity to advance your skills while working alongside a dedicated team of board-certified subspecialty radiologists. We can’t wait to meet you!
\n

Essential Functions
Demonstrates competency in the performance of job-related skills appropriate to his/her customer populations and departmental services
Schedules and coordinates tests, records test results, and prepares and maintains operational logs
Performs obstetric and gynecological scans, ultrasound techniques and examinations, abdominal scans, retroperitoneal scans, and radiation therapy localization
Calibrates and maintains equipment and orders supplies when needed
Familiar with standard concepts, practices and procedures. Relies on limited experience and judgment to plan and accomplish goals
Works under general supervision
A certain degree of creativity and latitude is required

Other Duties

title: Customs Brokerage Supervisor
description_text:
CUSTOMS BROKERAGE SUPERVISOR


About the Organization
We are a logistics and trade solutions company with a team of over 300 specialists dedicated to fulfilling customer supply chain needs. Our focus includes customs brokerage, domestic and international transportation, trade compliance, education, and consulting.


Our business practices are guided daily by three core values: ENRICH purposefully, CARE personally, and DELIVER professionally. We are an employee-centric organization that prioritizes our people and our customers. This commitment has earned us the "Great Place To Work" certification for the past eleven years.


Essential Duties & Responsibilities

Supervise, train, and coach Entry-Writing/CHB staff.

Assign accounts and distribute workload across the team.

Measure performance of staff and address any emerging issues.

Support local branch Import Transportation teams with brokerage requirements.

Troubleshoot freight issues with US Customs and other government agencies.

Enhance department performance in collaboration with the Customs Brokerage Manager.

Complete Entry work and audit files for accuracy as needed.

Oversee billing processes and monitor dwell time for all files.

Improve procedures and streamline the Entry department workflow.

On-board new clients according to sales or client-specific SOPs.

Verify accuracy of US Customs information on all documentation.

Execute projects and provide support as assigned by management.




Desired Skills & Experience


Accreditation: Certified Customs Specialist (CCS) required; Licensed Customs Broker preferred.

Experience: Minimum of 5 years working with US Customs and other government agencies.

Regulatory Knowledge: Thorough understanding of US Customs rules and regulations.

Leadership: Prior experience in personnel supervision and/or training.



Employee Characteristics

Self-motivated and capable of effective prioritization.

Highly organized with the ability to multi-task and maintain attention to detail.

Problem-solver capable of resolving issues across all aspects of the position.

Adaptable to changing work environments.

Objective leader who can effectively supervise a diverse team.


\n

\n

title: Clinic Success Manager
description_text:
Ensure Integrity. Enhance Satisfaction. Elicit Positivity. Bloomwell Autism Therapy is looking for a charismatic and driven leader to join our team as the Clinic Success Manager. The Clinic Success Manager ensures that everyone—the teammates, the patients and the parents—at a clinic is successful.
They are responsible for all non-clinical aspects of the clinic and for creating an efficient, productive and fun environment for our teammates and patients. 

This role is the second-in-command at the clinic and is the Clinical Director’s right hand. They are a team that works together to make sure the clinic as a whole is successful. 
\n

How We Support our Teammates
Competitive compensation with growth opportunities!
Ample PTO- 4 weeks of PTO are accrued throughout the year!
Ongoing Training and Development – We provide ongoing personal and professional development through our Boost program.
Unlimited Career Potential –We offer clear professional milestones to build your career.
Unmatched Benefits – We offer all our teammates a 401(k) plan with match, free short-term disability insurance, free life Insurance, access to mental health support, and more. 
Teammate Recognition through our partnership with Bonusly.
Holistic Perks Program: We provide thoughtful perks that focus on the whole individual such as: ClassPass membership, university program tuition discount, childcare subsidy and more. 

How You Will Nurture Your Clinic
Scheduling new patient assessments and family tours.
Handling new patient onboarding and scheduling.
Monitoring patient and teammate attendance.
Ensuring consistent and timely session note conversion.
Leading the clinic's morale committee by supporting in the organization of off-site team building activities, happy hours, and other teammate engagement activities.
Supporting the CD in budget oversight, teammate hiring, new-hire training, and teammate performance management initiatives. 
Planning community-focused events, caregiver/patient events and team building events each quarter.
Managing the clinic’s schedule for ongoing schedules, callouts, and coverage needs.
Ensuring proper maintenance of the facility, technology, and therapy materials to support seamless service delivery.
Conducting regular team meetings to communicate goals, updates, and performance insights.
Assisting in hiring, training, and onboarding new staff to ensure a strong team foundation.
Providing coaching and mentorship to staff, promoting professional growth and development.
Tracking clinic revenue, expenses, and financial performance in collaboration with leadership.

What You Need to Have
A 4-year degree from an accredited university required.
At least 2+ years of experience working in a healthcare environment strongly preferred.
Experience working in the ABA industry strongly preferred.
Excellent attention to detail skills.
Impeccable interpersonal skills as you will be interacting daily with families and teammates.
At least 1 year experience managing or handling oversight of a large team of hourly employees.
Ability to work 7:00am-3:00pm Monday - Friday and occasionally on the evenings or weekends for a community event.


\n
$60,000 - $65,000 a year
\n
Who We Are

We are Bloomwell ABA, a devoted and compassionate, multi-disciplinary group of clinicians that is creating a world where every child with autism gets the care and support they deserve. Energized by our mission to offer life-changing services to children and families affected by autism and unparalleled opportunities for our team across America, we are dedicated to advancing the state of autism care through best-in-class training, innovative treatment models and meaningful outcomes measurement. 
What We Live By

We are guided by our four core values: 

Championing the mission. We are committed to remaining laser-focused on our mission and doing whatever we can to achieve that for the children, families and team members that we serve. 
Supporting each other. Dealing with autism can be difficult for anyone. That is why we will do our best to support and encourage everyone around us. 
Serving with integrity. We will always prioritize doing what is right for our children, families and team members and be transparent and honest with ourselves and others. 
Pursuing growth. Our understanding of autism is constantly evolving and the number of people that are affected by it increases every day. We know that we cannot sit still and must do our best to innovate and grow. 

We Value Diversity, Equity, Inclusion and Belonging. We cannot wait to hear more about you, your experience, your motivators, and your dedication to our great mission.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Clinic Success Manager is responsible for all non-clinical aspects of the clinic, ensuring a productive and enjoyable environment for teammates and patients. They support the Clinical Director in various operational tasks and lead initiatives to enhance clinic morale and community engagement.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a 4-year degree and at least 2 years of experience in a healthcare environment, preferably in the ABA industry. Strong interpersonal skills and experience managing a large team are also required.

title: Account Manager Assistant
description_text:
We are Dulcedo

At Dulcedo, talent management is not about volume - it’s about strategy, structure, and long-term vision. We are a full-service talent management agency specializing in the representation of creators, influencers, athletes, models, and entertainment talent across North America and internationally. Our mission is to build scalable, sustainable careers for our talent while delivering white-glove service to the brands and partners who collaborate with them. We believe in strong systems, thoughtful recruitment, and intentional growth, not short-term wins or reactive hiring.

Dulcedo is the largest multidisciplinary talent management group in Canada and a recognized leader across the US and North American markets. We manage the global careers of 500+ models, hundreds of Olympic and professional athletes, and a growing roster of top-tier digital creators, influencers, and streamers. With offices in Montreal and Toronto and an extensive network across Los Angeles, New York, Miami, and beyond, Dulcedo operates at the intersection of talent, culture, and entertainment.

At the core of Dulcedo’s values and recruitment process is a strong commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion. Our current workforce is a celebration of diverse ethnic backgrounds, races, gender identities, sexual orientations, abilities, and lived experiences. Our continued mission is to recruit, support, and grow talent in a way that ensures equal opportunities for all applicants.

Above all, Dulcedo is a people-first organization. Our team is made up of ambitious, collaborative, and high-performing individuals who care deeply about culture, integrity, and impact. Proudly certified as a Great Place to Work, we foster an environment where people are empowered to grow, challenge themselves, and do meaningful work alongside industry-leading talent.


The Role At A Glance
As our new Influencer - Account Manager Assistant intern, you’ll be assisting with a variety of tasks that contribute to talent scouting and overall campaign execution at Dulcedo. Your profile should reflect an individual who is detail-oriented, proactive, and passionate about influencer marketing. You should have a strong interest in social media, talent development, and influencer-brand collaborations. This internship offers an excellent opportunity to learn and develop skills in talent recruitment and campaign coordination!

Your Role In Action
- Act as a middle man between different key stakeholders such as Talent Agents, Brands and Dulcedo Talents. 
- Help maintain continuous engagement with talents and brands throughout and beyond collaborations, offering support and guidance to enhance and nurture relationships.
- Support outbound PR pitching efforts for gifting and events by drafting outreach emails and assisting with templates.
- Assist social media strategizing for talents
- Maintain up-to-date records of partnership tracking and performance insights

What You’re Bringing With You
- Recent graduates or young professionals with a Bachelor’s Degree in a related field (PR, marketing, business, or communications).
- Strong organizational and time management skills offering great work ethics. 
- While bilingualism in French and English is considered a huge asset, it is sufficient to be fluent in English.
- Passion for the creator economy and influencer marketing (Bonus if in fitness and beauty niches)
- Knowledge and keen interest in social media marketing.
- Understanding of the world of public relations, collaborations and sponsorships.
- Strong desire to kick off a career in the world of talent management and influencer marketing.

Parameters & Perks!
- Duration: 16 weeks
- Schedule: Full-time (30-40 hours per week)
- Compensation: While our internships are unpaid, we offer a $500 stipend upon successful completion of the 16-week program.
- Full-Time Opportunity: This decision will be contingent on the successful completion of the internship, mutual agreement among all stakeholders and current hiring needs. 
- Location: Hybrid work environment, allowing you to get the best of worlds!
- Freestyle Fridays: Wrap up work at 2:00 PM every Friday
- A tight-knit team that thrives on respect, teamwork, and open communication.
- Startup Energy, Established Foundation: come work in an entrepreneurial, fast-moving environment backed by 15+ years of industry experience and the stability of a larger organization.
- Gain valuable, real-world marketing experience with direct involvement in projects that make an impact.

As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to making sure everyone feels included, supported, and valued. Should you require any accommodations during the application process, please write to talentacquisition@dulcedo.com and we will adjust the process to your needs.
\n

\n
ai_core_responsibilities: Assist with talent scouting and campaign execution, acting as a liaison between talent agents, brands, and talents. Maintain engagement with talents and brands, support PR pitching efforts, and assist in social media strategizing.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should be recent graduates or young professionals with a Bachelor's Degree in a related field. A strong interest in influencer marketing and social media, along with organizational skills, is essential.

title: Pediatric Occupational Therapist (OT)– Flexible Scheduling
description_text:
At MedCare Pediatric, we’re committed to transforming lives through compassionate, patient-centered care. As a trusted provider of home health services, we bring therapy directly into homes, daycares, and schools—where patients feel safest and most supported. Our mission is to empower individuals of all ages to reach their fullest potential by delivering exceptional therapeutic services grounded in integrity, collaboration, and excellence.

As a Home Health Occupational Therapist, you’ll provide personalized, one-on-one care in the environments where patients thrive. Our structured scheduling allows for meaningful interactions, focused treatment sessions, and detailed documentation—while offering the flexibility to support your work-life balance. You’ll join a collaborative, purpose-driven team that values professional development and equips you with the tools to make a lasting difference in the lives of the children and families we serve.

** Sign-On Bonus available for Part-Time and Full-Time Caseload **
\n

Occupational Therapist Qualifications and Responsibilities:
Graduate of an accredited school program of Occupational Therapy (OT)- new grads are welcome to apply!
Licensed to practice as an Occupational Therapist (OT) in Texas by anticipated start date
Experience in pediatrics and bilingual are a plus but necessarily required
Performs in depth patient evaluation and develops treatment plans and goals as indicated for patients based on their current level of functioning and potential for improvement
Evaluates patients’ needs and administers therapy to preserve and improve abilities for independent function, such as range of motion, strength, tolerance, coordination, vision, behavioral management skills, social skills, academics, and activities of daily living.
Implements treatment plan, provides state of the art Occupational Therapy services in accordance with the Plan of Treatment (POT) approved by the physician
Educate and train families about various OT techniques and strategies
Collaborate with the MedCare team to ensure quality care
Open to supervise assistants is a plus but not necessarily required

Transform Lives as a Home Health Occupational Therapist at MedCare Pediatric

Occupational Therapist Benefits & Schedule:
Flexible schedule options: Full-Time, Part-Time, and PRN openings available
Choose your own days and hours
New hires ramp up gradually
Caseload capped to support quality care
Support from our team of dedicated clinical supervisors and administrative staff.
Openings in the Greater Houston and all surrounding areas
Medical, Dental, and Vision insurance options
Employee Life, Short-Term and Long-Term Disability insurance options
Paid Time Off and Paid Holidays
Birthday Bonus
Company Issued Laptop
Professional License Reimbursement
CEU reimbursements
401K Plan
Employee Referral Bonus
After school and Weekend Stipends
Non taxed Travel reimbursement
Toy Allowance

title: Cookie Crew - Birmingham - Part Time

Have a hunger to succeed? A passion for the sweeter things in life? Got big dreams and a ceaseless imagination? Then we want to get to know YOU!

As a member of the Cookie Crew at our store located in Birmingham, you are the ultimate Insomniac – day or night! Not only are you the face of Insomnia Cookies for all our fans coming into the store, but you also get to bake all our awesome warm and delicious cookies as well.

Some of our Sweet Perks
Flexible work schedules – get work to work around you!
£12.21 per hour
Pension

Excellent training through the Cookie College

Bake our delicious cookies to perfection
Ensure fast, warm, and correct delivery orders are created and sent out
Cleaning, sanitising, and maintaining the bakery

Experience as within a customer service focused role preferred

Insomnia Cookies was founded in a college dorm room by then-student, Seth Berkowitz. Fast forward and Insomnia Cookies has become a cult brand in the US known for its rabid following of cookie lovers who crave Insomnia’s warm, delicious delivery all day and late into the night. With an expanding global footprint we are now here in the UK. We launched in Manchester in 2023, Sheffield in 2024 and are now coming to a city near you! title: Practice Manager
description_text:
For over 100 years, Straid Vets has been at the heart of the local community. We’re proud of our loyal clients, long-standing team members and genuinely close-knit atmosphere. As our practice continues to grow, we’re now looking for a caring, supportive and experienced Practice Manager to join our team and help shape the next chapter of our story.
This is a site-based, people-facing leadership role, perfect for someone who enjoys building strong relationships, supporting others to succeed, and making a real difference to both team and client experience.
This role is available for up to 40 hours per week, typically worked over 5 days, Monday to Friday. However, we are very open to discussing flexible/reduced hours or fewer days for the right person.
Why join Straid Vets?
Set in the beautiful Chiltern Hills, Beaconsfield offers the perfect blend of countryside living and city convenience. With fantastic shops, cafés and direct trains to London in under 30 minutes, it’s an ideal place to live, work and unwind.
Inside the practice, you’ll find a welcoming and collaborative team of vets, nurses, animal care assistants and receptionists who genuinely enjoy working together. We also make space for you to recharge, with comfortable staff areas, a quiet admin office, and those all-important weekly fruit and snacks alongside unlimited tea and coffee.
Caring doesn’t stop at our doors, we proudly support local charities including Alley Cat Rescue and Cats Protection.
Proud to Be Part of IVC Evidensia
At IVC Evidensia, we’re driven by the passion and commitment of our people – across clinical, operational, and support teams. With over 2,500 clinics and hospitals in 20 countries and around 41,000 employees, we work together to care for around 25,000 animals every day. United by a shared purpose – Healthy Animals, Happy Owners – we’re proud to champion long-term animal health and welfare through the dedication of every individual across our international network.
Key Responsibilities:
Working closely with the Director of Practice and Clinical Services, Lead Vet, Head Receptionist, and IVC Central Support teams, you will play a key role in ensuring the smooth day to day running of the practice while enhancing both team and client experience. Your responsibilities include:
*Leading, supporting, and motivating the practice team to foster a positive and collaborative working environment
*Maintaining practice standards and ensuring compliance with RCVS guidelines
*Financial management, including: Managing a practice budget, Stock control, Initiating capital expenditure requests, Supporting payroll production and queries
*Human Resources responsibilities, including: Line management and Appraisals
*Oversight of building and equipment maintenance
*Ensuring Health & Safety compliance with team support
*Recruitment and monitoring of mandatory and ongoing training compliance
*Acting as the first point of contact for IT queries (with support from a central IT function)
*Handling and resolving client complaints in a timely and professional manner
*Attending and leading practice and area meetings
What We’re Looking For
We’re seeking a confident and approachable leader with a genuine passion for people and practice culture. Previous practice management experience is desirable, but full training and support will be provided.
You’ll thrive here if you:
*Enjoy leading and developing others
*Communicate clearly and build strong relationships
*Are proactive, organised and solutions-focused
*Feel comfortable in a busy, fast-paced environment
*Take pride in creating a positive, efficient workplace
*Share ideas and lead by example
If you’re looking for a role where you can truly make an impact, leading a friendly, dedicated team in a community-focused practice, we’d love to hear from you.
Apply today and help us continue caring for our community.
\n

\n
£0 - £45,000 a year
Up to £45,000 DOE
\n

title: General Manager - Latham
description_text:
JETSET Pilates General Managers are committed to the growth and development of their team and are passionate about sharing the brand and workout with others. The General Manager is a strong leader who is dedicated to cultivating relationships within the studio, as well as role modeling all of the qualities and attributes of an exceptional instructor. 

The General Manager is expected to undergo the JETSET Pilates training program and instructs an average of 8 classes per week. The expectation of the General Manager is that in addition to instructing 8 classes per week on average, they are in the studio during class times for a minimum of 25 classes each week. 

The General Manager is a multi-faceted role responsible for all aspects of studio performance, growth, and people management in the studio. They are accountable for ensuring that all areas of the business are fulfilled and are driven by achieving goals and continuing growth.
\n

Duties and Responsibilities
Work closely with leadership to manage day-to-day operations of the studio and implement company-wide strategies, policies, and procedures.
Ensure the delivery of positive customer service and a fitness experience consistent with the JETSET Pilates service standards.
Run reports and analyze metrics on key performance indicators, including sales, utilization, retention, expenses, and payroll.
Develop and execute local marketing initiatives to drive client acquisition and exceed studio-specific goals.
Ensure all client inquiries, issues, and concerns receive a positive and timely response.
Foster relationships with the local health, fitness, and wellness communities as well as key influencers and potential corporate partners.
Create, monitor, and maintain class and staff schedules.
Maintain inventory for supplies, retail, and beverages as needed.
Plan and host team meetings and social events. 
Oversee hiring of instructors and studio leads and ensure career growth
Drive studio performance on metrics like number of classes, utilization, first visits, and memberships

\n
ai_core_responsibilities: The General Manager is responsible for managing day-to-day operations of the studio, ensuring positive customer service, and driving studio performance. They also oversee hiring, staff scheduling, and local marketing initiatives.

title: Software Engineer in Test
description_text:
About zaimler

AI agents can't reason over data they don't understand. Enterprise data today is fragmented across dozens of systems with no shared context, meaning, or structure, and that's why most enterprise AI is failing. The shift from copilots to autonomous agents is creating an entirely new infrastructure layer, and we're building it.

zaimler is the context infrastructure for the agentic era: a platform that automatically discovers domain knowledge, maps relationships, and gives AI agents the semantic understanding to operate with precision at scale. Imagine knowledge graphs that support real-time inference, built for systems that need to reason, not just retrieve.

zaimler was founded by Biswajit Das (ex-VP Engineering, Truera), a Data Infra veteran and former Chief Architect at Visa, and Sofus Macskassy (ex-Director of Engineering, LinkedIn), who built one of the largest knowledge graphs in production in the industry at LinkedIn. We're a small, senior team at the seed stage, deploying with major enterprises across insurance, travel, and technology. If you want to build infrastructure that the next decade of AI runs on, we'd love to talk.

The Role

We're looking for a Software Engineer in Test to own the quality and reliability of zaimler's platform from the ground up. Our infrastructure handles real-time knowledge graph construction, semantic inference, and data ingestion across complex enterprise environments, systems where correctness isn't optional and failures are expensive. You'll build the testing infrastructure, performance benchmarks, and integration validation that let us ship with confidence as we scale across production deployments.

This is not a QA role. You'll write more code than most software engineers, and the systems you build will be as critical as the platform itself.
\n

What You'll Do
Design and build end-to-end test infrastructure for zaimler's backend services, APIs, and data pipelines
Own performance and load testing: define benchmarks, build harnesses, and establish baselines for latency, throughput, and resource consumption under production-realistic conditions
Build and maintain integration test suites that validate correctness across the full stack, from data ingestion through knowledge graph construction to inference endpoints
Develop automated regression and smoke test pipelines integrated into CI/CD, ensuring every deploy is validated before it reaches enterprise customers
Identify reliability risks early by instrumenting systems, analyzing failure modes, and building chaos/fault-injection tests for critical paths
Collaborate directly with infrastructure and ML engineers to define testability requirements for new systems before they're built, not after

Must-haves:
3+ years of experience in software engineering, test engineering, or a related role
Strong software engineering fundamentals: you write production-grade code, not just test scripts
Deep experience with backend and API testing: REST/gRPC, async workflows, distributed systems
Hands-on experience building performance and load testing frameworks (e.g., Locust, k6, Gatling, or custom tooling)
Proficiency in Python; familiarity with Go or Java is a plus
Experience with CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or similar) and containerized environments (Docker, Kubernetes)
Understanding of data pipeline testing, validating correctness, consistency, and completeness across ETL or streaming systems

Nice-to-haves:
Experience testing graph databases, knowledge graphs, or semantic/ML inference systems
Familiarity with observability tooling (Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog) and using metrics to drive test strategy
Background in infrastructure-as-code and environment provisioning for test environments
Experience at a startup or early-stage company where you built testing practices from scratch

Working style:
You care about quality as a property of the system, not a phase in the process
You're comfortable defining what "good" looks like when there's no existing playbook
You'd rather automate yourself out of repetitive work than do it twice

Compensation & Benefits
Competitive salary benchmarked to the Bengaluru market
Meaningful early-stage equity
Health insurance
Flexible PTO
On-site in Bengaluru

\n
We value builders over résumés. If this role excites you but you don't check every box, we still want to hear from you. zaimler is an equal opportunity employer.

We value builders over résumés. If this role excites you but you don't check every box, we still want to hear from you. zaimler is an equal opportunity employer.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Software Engineer in Test will design and build end-to-end test infrastructure for zaimler's backend services and data pipelines. They will also own performance and load testing, build integration test suites, and collaborate with engineers to define testability requirements.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have 3+ years of experience in software engineering or a related role, with strong fundamentals in software engineering and deep experience in backend and API testing. Proficiency in Python and experience with CI/CD pipelines and containerized environments are also required.

title: Backend Engineering Intern (Summer 2026)
description_text:
About zaimler

AI agents can't reason over data they don't understand. Enterprise data today is fragmented across dozens of systems with no shared context, meaning, or structure, and that's why most enterprise AI is failing. The shift from copilots to autonomous agents is creating an entirely new infrastructure layer, and we're building it.

zaimler is the context infrastructure for the agentic era: a platform that automatically discovers domain knowledge, maps relationships, and gives AI agents the semantic understanding to operate with precision at scale. Imagine knowledge graphs that support real-time inference, built for systems that need to reason, not just retrieve.

zaimler was founded by Biswajit Das (ex-VP Engineering, Truera), a Data Infra veteran and former Chief Architect at Visa, and Sofus Macskassy (ex-Director of Engineering, LinkedIn), who built one of the largest knowledge graphs in production in the industry at LinkedIn. We're a small, senior team at the seed stage, deploying with major enterprises across insurance, travel, and technology. If you want to build infrastructure that the next decade of AI runs on, we'd love to talk.

About the Job

We are looking for a few interns to join us either part-time through the year or full-time for the summer. The ideal candidate should have an interest and some experience in backend systems, APIs, and data infrastructure. In this internship, you'll work directly on the services and systems that power zaimler's platform, building backend components that handle data ingestion, knowledge graph construction, and real-time inference at enterprise scale. You will get hands-on experience designing and building production systems that process large volumes of heterogeneous enterprise data.
\n

What You Will Be Doing
Build and extend backend services and APIs that power zaimler's core platform
Work on data ingestion pipelines that process and transform enterprise data from heterogeneous sources
Develop and optimize database queries and data access patterns for graph and relational datastores
Write tests and contribute to CI/CD pipelines to ensure reliability across deployments

title: Tile Shop Technician
description_text:
Zeeco fosters a robust work environment where employees, from engineering teams to support groups, benefit from a strong culture, trained leadership, and opportunities for growth. Zeeco collaborates with the biggest names in the industry, enabling individuals to broaden their skills alongside teams of subject matter experts. 

Job Summary:

The Tile Shop Technician is primarily responsible for pouring, assembling, and disassembling tile molds in a manufacturing environment. This role supports daily tile shop operations through safe, efficient mold preparation and handling. As business needs require, the technician will also assist with refractory installation and repair work at customer sites or internal facilities. The position requires strong mechanical aptitude, attention to safety, and the ability to perform physically demanding work in industrial environments.
\n

Primary Responsibilities:
Pour, assemble, and disassemble tile molds
Operate hand and power tools including impact wrenches, grinders, sanding tools, and tile sanding rocks
Prepare molds for production, including cleaning, inspection, and minor repairs
Assist with handling and movement of molds and materials within the tile shop
Follow work instructions and complete assigned tasks to meet production deadlines
Maintain a clean, organized, and safe tile shop work area
Perform basic preventive maintenance on tools and tile shop equipment
Maintain regular and reliable attendance

Secondary Responsibilities: 
Assist with refractory lining, relining, and repair of furnaces, ladles, tundishes, and related equipment
Support refractory installation methods including bricklaying, pouring, ramming, pumping, gunite, ceramic fiber, and firebrick installation
Assist with cleaning and preparing equipment for refractory repair and post-mortem tear outs
Operate refractory-related tools and equipment, including brick saws and material handling equipment
Support preventive maintenance, inventory tracking, and documentation related to refractory tools and materials

Qualifications:
Experience in a manufacturing or industrial environment
Tile/mold shop experience preferred; refractory experience a plus
Basic mechanical aptitude and willingness to learn
Ability to safely operate hand and power tools
Forklift certification preferred, or equivalent experience
Willingness to work outdoors and at customer sites as needed

Physical Requirements:
Ability to stand and work on feet for extended periods
Ability to lift 55–75 pounds
Frequent walking, bending, kneeling, crouching, and stair climbing
Ability to work in environments with extreme temperatures, high noise levels, fumes, airborne particles, and outdoor weather conditions
Ability to wear required PPE, including hearing and respiratory protection

\n
Working at Zeeco means being part of a people-centric culture and environment built on mutual respect, personal encouragement, professional integrity, and limitless opportunity. Join Zeeco and embark on a fulfilling journey where innovation, collaboration, and personal and professional growth converge.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Tile Shop Technician is responsible for pouring, assembling, and disassembling tile molds in a manufacturing environment. This role supports daily tile shop operations through safe, efficient mold preparation and handling.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have experience in a manufacturing or industrial environment, with tile/mold shop experience preferred. A basic mechanical aptitude and willingness to learn are essential, along with the ability to safely operate hand and power tools.

title: Frontend Engineering Intern (Summer 2026)
description_text:
About zaimler

AI agents can't reason over data they don't understand. Enterprise data today is fragmented across dozens of systems with no shared context, meaning, or structure, and that's why most enterprise AI is failing. The shift from copilots to autonomous agents is creating an entirely new infrastructure layer, and we're building it.

zaimler is the context infrastructure for the agentic era: a platform that automatically discovers domain knowledge, maps relationships, and gives AI agents the semantic understanding to operate with precision at scale. Imagine knowledge graphs that support real-time inference, built for systems that need to reason, not just retrieve.

zaimler was founded by Biswajit Das (ex-VP Engineering, Truera), a Data Infra veteran and former Chief Architect at Visa, and Sofus Macskassy (ex-Director of Engineering, LinkedIn), who built one of the largest knowledge graphs in production in the industry at LinkedIn. We're a small, senior team at the seed stage, deploying with major enterprises across insurance, travel, and technology. If you want to build infrastructure that the next decade of AI runs on, we'd love to talk.

About the Job

We are looking for a few interns to join us either part-time through the year or full-time for the summer. The ideal candidate should have an interest and some experience in frontend development and building interfaces for complex, data-rich applications. In this internship, you'll work on the product surfaces that enterprise data teams use to explore, validate, and interact with zaimler's semantic graph systems. You will get hands-on experience building UI components that make complex data infrastructure intuitive and usable.
\n

What You Will Be Doing
Build and improve frontend components and pages in zaimler's web application
Develop interactive visualizations for knowledge graphs, data relationships, and pipeline status
Work with designers and product engineers to translate requirements into polished, responsive interfaces
Integrate frontend components with backend APIs and real-time data sources
Contribute to frontend tooling, component libraries, and design system foundations

Prior Experience
Pursuing a Bachelors or Masters in CS
Startup internship experience is highly preferred
Proficiency in JavaScript/TypeScript and React
Interest in building complex, data-dense interfaces (dashboards, graph visualizations, developer tools)
Understanding of HTML, CSS, and responsive design principles
Familiarity with state management patterns
Strong eye for detail and care about user experience
Experience with version control (Git) and collaborative development workflows
Great communication skills and a team player

Nice to Have
Experience with data visualization libraries (D3.js, Recharts, or similar)
Familiar with graph visualization
Experience with Next.js or other React frameworks
Familiar with design tools (Figma, Storybook)
Interest in developer tooling, infrastructure UIs, or enterprise product design

\n
We value builders over résumés. If this role excites you but you don't check every box, we still want to hear from you. zaimler is an equal opportunity employer.
ai_core_responsibilities: Interns will build and improve frontend components and pages in zaimler's web application, as well as develop interactive visualizations for knowledge graphs and data relationships. They will work closely with designers and product engineers to create polished, responsive interfaces.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should be pursuing a Bachelor's or Master's in Computer Science and have proficiency in JavaScript/TypeScript and React. Experience with data visualization libraries and a strong eye for detail are also preferred.

title: Full Stack Engineering Intern (Summer 2026)
description_text:
About zaimler

AI agents can't reason over data they don't understand. Enterprise data today is fragmented across dozens of systems with no shared context, meaning, or structure, and that's why most enterprise AI is failing. The shift from copilots to autonomous agents is creating an entirely new infrastructure layer, and we're building it.

zaimler is the context infrastructure for the agentic era: a platform that automatically discovers domain knowledge, maps relationships, and gives AI agents the semantic understanding to operate with precision at scale. Imagine knowledge graphs that support real-time inference, built for systems that need to reason, not just retrieve.

zaimler was founded by Biswajit Das (ex-VP Engineering, Truera), a Data Infra veteran and former Chief Architect at Visa, and Sofus Macskassy (ex-Director of Engineering, LinkedIn), who built one of the largest knowledge graphs in production in the industry at LinkedIn. The ideal candidate should have an interest and some experience across both frontend and backend development. In this internship, you'll build features end-to-end, from the API layer through the UI, working on the product that enterprise data teams use to interact with zaimler's semantic graph platform. What You Will Be Doing

\n

title: Head of Customer Lifecycle & Engagement
description_text:
Our Story
Hello there. We’re Zopa.
We started our journey back in 2005, building the first ever peer-to-peer lending company. Fast forward to 2020 and we launched Zopa Bank. A bank that listens to what our customers don’t like about finance and does the opposite. We’re redefining what it feels like to work in finance. Our vision for a new era of banking puts people front and centre — we’ve built a business that empowers everyone to aim high, every day, to move finance forward. Find out more about our fantastic offerings at Zopa.com! 

We’re incredibly proud of our achievements and none of it would be possible without the amazing team here. It’s not just industry awards we’re winning, we’ve also been named in the top three UK’s Most Loved Workplaces. 

If you embrace unconventional challenges, are unafraid to think differently and are driven to make an outsized impact, you’ll thrive here at Zopa, so join us, and make it count. Want to see us in action? Follow us on Instagram @zopalife 

Zopa is building the home of money — and we’re at a turning point in how we engage, retain and grow our customers. 

We’re hiring a leader to build and lead our customer lifecycle capability end-to-end: strategy, execution, technology and team. This role owns how we communicate with customers across their full relationship with Zopa — from activation through to retention, cross-sell and long-term value

This is a hands-on leadership role in a regulated, data-rich environment — ideal for someone who has built or transformed a CRM / lifecycle function before and wants to do it again with real ownership and impact. If you’re excited by complexity, imperfect starting points, and the chance to shape how millions of customers experience a modern digital bank — we'd love to hear from you!
\n

What you’ll be doing:
Set CRM strategy 
Define a multi-year CRM and customer engagement strategy aligned to Zopa’s growth ambitions 
Translate strategy into a clear roadmap, priorities and success metrics 
Build scalable customer engagement 
Design lifecycle-led, personalised journeys across email, app, push, SMS and in-product channels 
Own segmentation, targeting and next-best-action approaches 
Own CRM tech & data direction 
Lead CRM platform evolution and martech migration in partnership with Product, Data and Tech 
Ensure data quality, governance and measurement underpin all CRM activity 
Lead & grow the team 
Lead and develop a high-performing CRM team 
Evolve team structure and capabilities to meet future needs 
Drive outcomes 
Own CRM performance against engagement, cross-sell and customer value metrics 
Embed test-and-learn and continuous optimisation 

About you:
Leadership experience in CRM /customer engagement within a digital, data-driven business
Proven track record of building or transforming CRM capabilities
Experience owning commercial outcomes, not just execution
Exceptional strategic and analytical thinker
Deep understanding of CRM platforms, data and automation
Confident influencing senior stakeholders
Experienced people leader through change
Clear communicator with sound judgement

\n
At Zopa we value flexible ways of working. 
We value face-to-face collaboration and a good work-life balance. This hybrid role requires you to come to our London office 2-3 days a week.
You'll also have the option of working from abroad for up to 120 days a year!* But no matter where you are, we’ll make sure you’ve got everything you need to thrive, both in your work and home life, from day one.

*Subject to having the right to work in the country of choice

Diversity Statement
Zopa is proud to offer a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and backgrounds leads to better products for our customers and a unique company culture for our people. We are made up of nearly 50 nationalities, have a DE&I forum made up of Zopians wanting to make a difference and we are proud of our culture where everyone can bring their full self to work. Our approach to DE&I is reflected in our hiring process so please let us know if you require any reasonable adjustments.

ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have leadership experience in CRM/customer engagement within a digital, data-driven business and a proven track record of building or transforming CRM capabilities. Exceptional strategic and analytical thinking, along with a deep understanding of CRM platforms and data, is essential.

linkedin_org_locations: 20, Water Street, London, London E14 9QG, GB
4th Floor, WeWork Dalton Place, 29 John Dalton Street, Manchester, M2 6FW, GB
linkedin_org_description:
We’re Zopa, and we want to make money work better for you.

Our diverse team is united in their mission of creating simple, fair and honest financial products that have the customer’s needs at their heart. We’re proud that this dedication is reflected in our excellent rating on TrustPilot.

We’ve always been unapologetically honest with our customers, and value the same in return. Their feedback helps us shape what we build, so we can provide a bank fit for today, and for the future.

We’re not the new kids on the block though - we’ve been a pioneering force in finance for 16 years. In 2005, we built the first ever peer-to-peer (P2P) lending company, giving our customers access to loans built for real-life and intelligent investments backed by cutting-edge tech. In 2020, we launched Zopa Bank, meaning we could offer more – like fixed term savings backed by FSCS protection and a credit card to help customers take control of their finances.

We’ve lent out over £6 billion and are proud to have made money work better for over half a million people across the UK, whether they were looking to borrow or save.

title: Director of Marketing — Xsolla Partner Network & Lightstream
description_text:
ABOUT YOU
We are looking for a Director of Marketing who is strategic, operationally excellent, and deeply fluent in the creator economy to join our Xsolla Partner Network marketing team, supporting both Xsolla Partner Network and Lightstream. The best candidate thrives in a highly collaborative and fast-paced environment and is excited to translate strategy into execution, scale marketing operations, and drive measurable growth across creator-driven programs. Strong influencer/creator marketing expertise, cross-functional leadership, growth marketing fundamentals, and exceptional communication skills are essential. Experience in gaming, creator ecosystems, entertainment, or subscription-based products is highly valuable. The ability to manage complex workflows, orchestrate multi-team marketing initiatives, optimize performance, and guide team development will be key to your success. If you’re passionate about building impactful creator and influencer programs, driving growth through integrated marketing, and supporting developers and creators through innovative solutions, we would love to hear from you!

ABOUT US
Xsolla is a global commerce company with robust tools and services to help developers solve the inherent challenges of the video game industry. From indie to AAA, companies partner with Xsolla to help them fund, distribute, market, and monetize their games. Grounded in the belief in the future of video games, Xsolla is resolute in the mission to bring opportunities together and continually make new resources available to creators. Headquartered and incorporated in Los Angeles, California, Xsolla operates as the merchant of record and has helped over 1,500+ game developers reach more players and grow their businesses around the world. With more paths to profits and ways to win, developers have all the things needed to enjoy the game. For more information, visit xsolla.com.

\n

Responsibilities 
Strategic Partnership & Leadership
Co-develop and operationalize integrated marketing strategy for Xsolla Partner Network and Lightstream, supporting ecosystem-wide initiatives across Xsolla Mall, x.la and emerging verticals.
Translate strategic direction into campaign roadmaps, briefs, milestones, and measurable KPIs.
Ensure marketing execution supports key productivity drivers: creator acquisition, campaign velocity, operational efficiency, and influencer engagement.
Act as connective tissue between strategy and execution, ensuring high-quality delivery across all workstreams.
Provide thought leadership in creator engagement, product evolution, competitive positioning, and content strategy.Contribute to future marketing team design and phased expansion across CRM, Social, Community, and Content.
Influencer & Creator Marketing
Lead creator marketing programs from concept through execution and reporting.
Develop and scale creator partnerships across Twitch, YouTube, TikTok, and emerging platforms.
Architect multi-channel creator campaigns and negotiate partnerships aligned to growth objectives.
Partner with Product, Product Marketing, Creator Success, and BD to align creator programs with platform features, partner needs, and engagement workflows.
Build reporting models to measure ROAS/ROI, audience lift, attribution accuracy, and creator impact.
Integrated Growth Marketing
Collaborate with CRM, Social, and Community teams to build lifecycle and nurture flows that increase creator retention, Lightstream subscriptions, and Partner Network sign-ups.
Reintroduce and scale Partner Network and Lightstream’s social presence, ensuring consistency, brand integrity, and content velocity.
Partner with BI and Analytics teams to track funnel performance, creator activation, content attribution, and LTV.
Work with UA/Paid Media on pilot programs and creative testing.
Support content strategy across short-form, long-form, and campaign-specific content.
Cross-Functional Collaboration
Partner with Product and Product Marketing to translate creator insights into messaging, positioning, and roadmap alignment.
Support GTM readiness for new product features, Lightstream updates, and partner launches.
Work with BD and Product Marketing to deliver co-marketing programs that drive adoption and engagement.
Coordinate with PR, Events, Integrated Marketing, and Regional teams to ensure unified storytelling across channels.
Operational Excellence & Team Development
Own the marketing operating model across Partner Network and Lightstream, including campaign intake, prioritization, workflow design, GTM documentation, and approvals.
Drive execution across all marketing workstreams ensuring predictability and performance.
Manage multi-channel budgets (creator incentives, paid pilots, content production, CRM/tools, regional programs) aligned with KPI gates and ROI expectations.
Mentor and guide team members, fostering a culture of accountability, innovation, and collaboration.
Partner with Operations, BI, and Data to ensure reporting clarity, attribution accuracy, and performance visibility.

Qualification & Skills 
Required
10+ years of progressive marketing experience in gaming, creator marketing, entertainment, or related fields.
Demonstrated expertise in influencer/creator marketing, global program management, and performance measurement.
Experience collaborating with AAA studios, publishers, or high-profile gaming brands.
Strong foundation in growth, lifecycle, content, and social marketing.
Proficiency with GA4, Sprout, CRM systems, Mailchimp/Braze, and analytics dashboards.
Proven success managing complex cross-functional stakeholders and multi-channel budgets.
Exceptional written, verbal, and executive communication skills.
Preferred
Experience in hybrid B2B2C ecosystems (developer + creator).Exposure to SaaS or subscription-based product marketing.
Familiarity with global creator markets across Americas, EMEA, and APAC.
Experience building or leading small, high-performing teams in fast-paced environments.

\n
$150,000 - $180,000 a year
\n
BENEFITS
We are passionate about fostering a supportive environment for our team, so we prioritize the physical, mental, and emotional well-being of our employees and their families through a comprehensive Benefits Program. This includes 100% company-paid medical, dental, and vision plans, unlimited Flexible Time Off, and a personalized career roadmap for each employee. We invest in professional development through training and educational opportunities to ensure our team thrives both personally and professionally.

EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY STATEMENT
Xsolla is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other characteristic protected by law.We consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in accordance with the Fair Chance Act.

CRIMINAL HISTORY CONSIDERATION
For the Director of Marketing — Xsolla Partner Network & Lightstream, Xsolla will conduct a background check that may include the following:
Criminal history check
Employment verification
Education verification
Relevance to Job Responsibilities 
This background check is relevant because the role includes:
Accessing confidential company data
Managing budgets and financial information
Ensuring compliance with regulatory and operational requirements

RIGHTS UNDER THE FAIR CHANCE ACT
Applicants are encouraged to inquire about their rights under the Fair Chance Act.For questions regarding hiring practices, please contact careers@xsolla.com.By submitting your application, you consent to Xsolla processing your data in accordance with the Xsolla Privacy Notice for Job Applicants.Please direct any inquiries regarding your data privacy to careers@xsolla.com.

title: Programmatic Account Executive
locations_derived: New York, New York, United States
description_text:
Vevo is the world's leading music video network, connecting an ever-growing global audience to high quality music video content for more than a decade. Founded by Universal Music Group and Sony Music Entertainment in 2009, Vevo offers fans worldwide a vast array of premium content to choose from, showcasing official music videos alongside a constantly developing lineup of live performances and innovative original programming. From top superstars to rising new talents, Vevo brings incomparable cross-promotional support to artists across the musical spectrum, at every stage of their careers.

Vevo has consistently evolved over the past decade to lead within today's ever-changing media landscape, embracing partnerships with a number of leading distribution platforms to deliver extraordinary content within ad-supported environments. With more than 25B views across television, desktop and mobile devices each month, Vevo brings music videos to the world – when, where, and how fans want them. 

Vevo is looking for a rock star salesperson to sell Vevo’s music video programming platform to programmatic buyers across clients and agencies. The Account Executive is responsible for building relationships, driving revenue and closing business. 
This Account Executive will play a critical role within our Sales organization as automated buying and premium programmatic becomes an important part of Vevo’s offering to marketers. The individual will be responsible for his/her own sales efforts as well as providing support to the larger sales team in the region. A team player is a MUST!
As the Programmatic Account Executive, you will have the opportunity to help shape new capabilities, strategies, products and partnerships across the exploding programmatic, digital, and OTT ecosystem, all while working side by side with industry leaders both at Vevo and our partners. You will be challenged to grow and lead us in new and innovative directions, and together we will learn and build a business! 
This is a full-time position based at our New York City office.
\n

As a member of the team, you will: 
Develop, grow, and manage a book of business at the Agency/HoldCo level and In House Programmatic, with an emphasis on scaling Private Marketplace (PMP) demand
Consult with clients on their Programmatic CTV needs, and present innovative video solutions to meet their objectives, especially within data and custom curated biddable solutions
Support the sales team in acquiring new clients and renewals to grow revenue
Forecast/track/report sales performance, including budget, dates, and probability to close
Manage relationships with 3rd party vendors (Ex: SSP,DSP,DMP) – and help grow Vevo’s ad tech roadmap
Educate the Vevo sales and sales support teams on the evolving landscape of CTV, programmatic, and audience

This describes you:
You have established relationships with buyers across agencies, trading desks, DSPs, and client-side teams
Experience scaling Private Marketplace revenue and data driven solutions 
Passionate about CTV/OTT, programmatic, ad tech, digital advertising, and music
A good understanding of the programmatic marketplace as well as the CTV/OTT marketplace
Ability to lead, collaborate, and work autonomously in a dynamic start-up team environment with a positive demeanor
Ability to analyze clients’ business marketing objectives, brand or ROI goals, media buying cycles, metrics for success, etc.
Ability to travel across the Midwest
Not afraid to lead and push sales teams to understand the what, why and how of what’s possible
Have superior communication and client service skills, with a drive to close new business and revenue
Outgoing personality who loves to entertain clients at a variety of events and exclusive concerts

Requirements:
3 years experience in the media industry/sales
Strong understanding of advertising technology landscape
Strong understanding of the CTV/OTT marketplace
Strong negotiation & presentation skillsExcellent organizational, analytical, interpersonal, and communication (written and oral) skills
Ability to pivot based on real-time information and client needs
Excellent communication skills and attention to detail
Ability to work independently and problem solve as well as effectively collaborate with various teams
Must be nimble, not afraid to work hard and have fun along the way!

Interested? Great! You must know:
We're a fun, energetic, and tight-knit team
We really enjoy music and technology
We have excellent compensation and benefits packages
We have premier access to music content and new releases of original media content
We offer a 401k match

\n
$130,000 - $150,000 a year
\n

title: RPA Developer
description_text:
About This Role

We are looking for an RPA Developer who thrives on turning manual chaos into streamlined automation. In this role, you will lead the end-to-end journey of robotic process automation—from hunting for high-impact opportunities within business units to deploying and maintaining the bots that keep our operations humming. Your goal is to build reliable, scalable "digital workers" that allow our human teams to focus on the high-value work they do best.
\n

What You'll Do:
Architect End-to-End Solutions: Independently design, develop, and deploy RPA workflows that don't just work—they excel. You'll own the full lifecycle from the first line of code to the final production rollout.
Consult & Discover: Partner with cross-functional teams to "look under the hood" of their processes. You’ll analyze their workflows, determine if automation is feasible, and provide strategic recommendations to make their lives easier.
Keep the Gears Turning: Take ownership of "Bot Health." You’ll monitor, troubleshoot, and fine-tune existing automations to ensure they stay reliable and minimize any potential downtime.
Write the Playbook: Maintain high-quality documentation for every project. From design specs to testing protocols, you’ll ensure our automation library is "audit-ready" and easy to navigate.
Mentor & Influence: As a senior member of the initial development tier, you’ll share your "lessons learned" and best practices with the team, helping to foster a culture of continuous learning and technical excellence.
Stay Ahead of the Curve: Actively experiment with the latest RPA tools and industry trends, ensuring our automation stack remains modern and competitive.

Who You Are:
A Proven Builder: You have 1–2 years of hands-on experience in RPA development or a similar automation-focused role, with a solid grasp of the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC).
Tool-Agnostic & Tech-Savvy: You are proficient in major RPA platforms (like UiPath, Automation Anywhere, or Blue Prism) and have the scripting chops (Python, Java, or C#) to handle complex integrations.
An Analytical Thinker: You don't just follow instructions; you look at a process and see the logic gaps. You have a knack for identifying where automation can provide the highest ROI.
A Clear Communicator: You can take a complex technical workflow and explain it to a non-technical stakeholder in a way that makes sense and builds excitement.
A Problem Solver: When a bot breaks at 2:00 PM, you’re the one who enjoys the "detective work" required to find the root cause and implement a permanent fix.

\n
$75,000 - $95,000 a year
\n
ai_core_responsibilities: The RPA Developer will design, develop, and deploy RPA workflows while monitoring and maintaining existing automations. They will also partner with cross-functional teams to identify automation opportunities and ensure high-quality documentation.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 1-2 years of hands-on experience in RPA development and be proficient in major RPA platforms and scripting languages. Strong analytical thinking and communication skills are essential for this role.

title: Solution Consultant
description_text:
The Opportunity

You'll seize a pivotal opportunity to dramatically enhance the throughput of our Solutions Consulting team, directly impacting our ability to deliver high-quality SOWs and engage proactively in critical customer conversations. This role empowers you to articulate the value of our Services organization, expertly scope customer requirements, and craft commercially viable Statements of Work that drive significant business growth.

What You'll Do

As a Solutions Consultant, you'll extend the capacity of our dynamic Solutions Consulting team, becoming an indispensable asset in the development of crucial deal deliverables, including presentations and detailed Statements of Work. Beyond supporting existing engagements, you'll leverage your expertise to independently lead smaller, yet impactful, scoping engagements for our global prospects, ensuring precise alignment and successful project kick-offs. This dual focus means you'll not only contribute to accelerating our sales cycles, but also the end-to-end process of translating complex customer needs into actionable, commercially sound service proposals.

The Skills You'll Have

Consulting
+ Demonstrate strong evidence of successfully selling professional services within a software environment.
+ Possess an acute ability to discern true customer needs, translating unspoken requirements into actionable solutions.
+ Articulate the impact of various solution decisions and approaches on project timelines.
+ Proactively ask insightful discovery questions during customer conversations.

Consultative Sales and Solutioning
+ Translate complex customer needs into clear, commercially viable proposals, identifying unspoken requirements and potential risks.
+ Ensure all proposed solutions are not only effective, but also align with commercial viability and strategic objectives.
+ Bring proven experience working within a SaaS company or as a SaaS System Integrator.
+ Demonstrate a track record of successfully selling and scoping services engagements, ideally within a Salesforce SI or Salesforce-based SaaS environment.
+ Articulate a clear point-of-view on how an implementation team strategically adds value throughout the customer journey.

Project Management
+ Ask in-depth, clarifying questions to fully comprehend customer needs and align them with our service offerings.
+ Manage your responsibilities across multiple deal cycles and customer engagements with a disciplined, project-management-oriented approach.
+ Connect explicit customer requests with the broader implications for our services, ensuring comprehensive solutions.
+ Exhibit a high-agency mindset, proactively driving initiatives with minimal oversight.
\n

Within 90 Days, You'll
Obtain comprehensive product and operational business knowledge, enabling you to provide direct, 2-up internal support to Solution Consulting peers by developing SOW drafts, leveraging AI and internal assets for information synthesis, and preparing implementation proposal options.
Grasp our implementation process, product capabilities, and scoping methodology sufficiently to independently lead initial scope discussions with Sitetracker prospects.

Within 180 Days, You'll
Consistently lead and successfully close a portfolio of scoping engagements for EMEA prospects, contributing directly to regional sales targets.
Become a recognized internal expert, routinely developing high-quality SOWs and strategic presentations that significantly accelerate deal velocity for the broader Solution Consulting team.
Proactively identify and begin implementing process improvements in deal deliverable creation, contributing to reduced average turnaround times for core team deliverables.

Within 365 Days, You'll
Significantly expand our SC sales support capacity across EMEA, becoming a cornerstone for regional growth and successful service delivery.
Drive a measurable, global reduction in average turnaround times for core SC deal deliverables, setting new benchmarks for efficiency.
Forge strong internal partnerships, becoming an invaluable resource and mentor for peers, continually elevating the team's collective expertise and output.

\n
ai_core_responsibilities: As a Solutions Consultant, you will enhance the Solutions Consulting team's throughput by developing deal deliverables and leading scoping engagements. You will translate complex customer needs into actionable service proposals and contribute to accelerating sales cycles.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have experience selling professional services in a software environment and a proven track record in SaaS or Salesforce-based settings. Strong project management skills and the ability to discern customer needs are essential.

title: Legal Intern
description_text:
Make Your Mark as a Legal Intern at Storio group

About the role:

As a Legal Intern, you will be a vital member of our Legal Team supporting our European business as we scale. We operate at the intersection of technology and creativity, so you will work on a diverse range of projects from commercial law to AI and sustainability. This role offers a front-row seat to the legal mechanics of a pan-European leader, giving you hands-on experience in a fast-paced e-commerce environment.
\n

How you embrace curiosity daily:
Support legal operations across a wide variety of topics and tasks.
Research new European and UK legislation and prepare summary reports for the team.
Help develop our contract management system and other compliance tools.
Review legal questions from colleagues and research the latest legal literature.
Monitor and report on several internal registers to keep our records accurate.
Assist with corporate housekeeping by organizing and updating key company documents.

How you make your mark:
Help the legal team reach ambitious targets by bringing solutions and ideas to the table.
Ensure our contract management processes remain efficient as we grow.
Provide clear research that helps our leaders make informed decisions.
Manage sensitive information with integrity to build trust across the business.
Collaborate with stakeholders from different cultures to support our international brands.
Contribute to our high standards by keeping our corporate governance organized.

What you bring to the team:
A proactive attitude and the ability to solve problems with sound judgment.
Strong communication skills and the ability to relate to people across different departments.
A genuine interest in technology and the fast-paced world of e-commerce.
Fluency in English and a collaborative, team-first mindset.
Integrity and discretion when handling sensitive legal information.
Availability for a six-month internship based in the Netherlands.

What sets you apart:
You are currently enrolled in a Dutch university.
You have proficiency in another European language.
You are comfortable working in a hybrid environment and visiting our Amsterdam office three days a week.

\n
About us
At Storio Group, we help people hold onto life's moments. We make personalised photo products that turn fleeting memories into things you can keep, share, and re-live.

Every person at Storio Group helps create our products and shape our company. You will see the impact of your work daily. We invite you to make your mark on our business, products, and customers' lives.

We act with heart by putting people first and valuing diverse perspectives. We give our best and aim for high standards in all we do. We own our work, taking initiative to find solutions. We embrace curiosity, always learning and trying new things. We find the joy in our work and create a positive environment.

Equal Opportunities & Right to Work
Storio Group is an equal opportunity employer, celebrating diversity and fostering an inclusive environment. If you require reasonable adjustments during interviews please contact our HR team. 

Applicants must also have the legal right to work in the position's country without requiring sponsorship.
